errors.message: top level must be a mapping (collection: ..., playbooks: [...])

rows.yaml:
collection: SOC Playbooks
playbooks:
  - name: FortiGate Block or Allow IP
    description: >
      Prompts an analyst to enter an IP address and choose Block or Allow.
      On Block, calls FortiGate block_ip (Quarantine Based).
      On Allow, logs a set_variable note and exits.
    is_active: true
    steps:
      # ── Trigger ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
      - type: start
        name: Start
        next: Analyst IP Decision

      # ── Manual Input ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
      # kind: ipv4  →  strict IPv4 field (not free-text)
      # kind: select →  drop-down; values are lower-case strings compared
      #                 in the decision step below
      # Both options converge on the same decision step so the branch label
      # on the manual_input route doesn't affect routing logic.
      - type: manual_input
        name: Analyst IP Decision
        arguments:
          title: "Block or Allow an IP Address"
          description: >
            Enter the IP address you want to act on, then choose whether
            to Block it on FortiGate or Allow (no action) it.
          inputs:
            - name: target_ip
              kind: ipv4
              label: IP Address
              tooltip: "Enter the IPv4 address to block or allow."
              required: true
            - name: action
              kind: select
              label: Action
              tooltip: "Choose Block to quarantine on FortiGate, or Allow to skip."
              required: true
              options:
                - block
                - allow
        options:
          - display: Block
            primary: true
            next: Route on Action
          - display: Allow
            next: Route on Action

      # ── Decision ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
      # vars.steps.Analyst_IP_Decision.input.<field_name>  ← canonical path
      # for manual_input fields (spaces → underscores in step-name slug).
      - type: decision
        name: Route on Action
        conditions:
          - display: Block
            when: "{{ vars.steps.Analyst_IP_Decision.input.action == 'block' }}"
            next: Block IP on FortiGate
          - display: Else
            default: true
            next: Log Allow Decision

      # ── Block branch ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
      # Canonical op: block_ip  (confirmed from fortigate-firewall connector)
      # Method: Quarantine Based  →  params: ip_addresses, time_to_live, vdom(opt)
      # Mixing Policy Based params here would cause hidden-field errors.
      - type: connector
        name: Block IP on FortiGate
        arguments:
          connector: fortigate-firewall
          operation: block_ip
          config: ''
          params:
            method: "Quarantine Based"
            ip_addresses: "{{ vars.steps.Analyst_IP_Decision.input.target_ip }}"
            time_to_live: "12 Hour"
        next: Log Block Result

      - type: set_variable
        name: Log Block Result
        vars:
          block_status: "Blocked"
          actioned_ip: "{{ vars.steps.Analyst_IP_Decision.input.target_ip }}"
          fortigate_response: "{{ vars.steps.Block_IP_on_FortiGate }}"
        message:
          content: >
            FortiGate Block executed for IP
            {{ vars.steps.Analyst_IP_Decision.input.target_ip }}.
            Newly blocked: {{ vars.steps.Block_IP_on_FortiGate.newly_blocked }}.
          tags:
            - fortigate
            - block
            - automation
        next: End Block

      # ── Allow branch ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
      - type: set_variable
        name: Log Allow Decision
        vars:
          block_status: "Allowed"
          actioned_ip: "{{ vars.steps.Analyst_IP_Decision.input.target_ip }}"
          note: "Analyst chose Allow — no FortiGate action taken."
        message:
          content: >
            IP {{ vars.steps.Analyst_IP_Decision.input.target_ip }} was reviewed
            and marked Allowed. No FortiGate block was applied.
          tags:
            - fortigate
            - allow
            - analyst_decision
        next: End Allow

      # ── Terminators ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
      - type: end
        name: End Block

      - type: end
        name: End Allow

final_verify_ready_to_push.detail: last verify did NOT return ready_to_push=True
